title: Polystachya luteola
taxonomy: Polystachya luteola is classified within the plant kingdom as an orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and the subfamily Orchidoideae. Further classification places it in the tribe Vandeae and the subtribe Angraecinae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and morphology. The classification of Polystachya luteola is crucial for its identification and understanding of its evolutionary relationships.

intro: Polystachya luteola is a species of orchid native to tropical regions of Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may aid in treating various infections and reducing inflammation in the body. The bioactive constituents of Polystachya luteola include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
